title: A Divine Image
content:
Cruelty has a human heart,
    And Jealousy a human face;
    Terror the human form divine,
    And Secresy the human dress.

    The human dress is forged iron,
    The human form a fiery forge,
    The human face a furnace sealed,
    The human heart its hungry gorge.
meaning:
William Blake's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Divine Image"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
